title: You can't automate your way to intelligence
description: Guest on Liquidware Chats. Full cognitive stack walkthrough, VDI Delusion parallels, the invisible 80% of knowledge work, applications as compatibility layers, and why automating the bottom of the stack won't produce intelligence at the top.

title: The new cognitive stack
description: Closing keynote at DUCUG #28. Introduces the cognitive stack, demonstrates a live second brain, presents token economics, and maps the governance argument. Slides available.

title: Skills are all you need
description: Why plain-English skill files are replacing software, automations, and entire job categories. Skills appreciate; software depreciates.

title: 2026: The year your AI strategy becomes irrelevant
description: The invisible 80% framework applied to why corporate AI strategies miss what matters. Published in AI Journal.

title: Announcing brianmadden.ai: I just published my brain for you to merge into your AI
description: A structured knowledge repo served via MCP. Not content but infrastructure that compounds inside your AI system.

title: Understanding the cognitive stack: why your AI strategy is focused on the wrong layer
description: A five-layer model of how intelligence coordinates complex work. The enterprise AI industry is spending billions on the bottom two layers.

title: What will knowledge work be in 18 months? Look at what AI is doing to coding right now.
description: Software engineering is the leading indicator for all knowledge work. The five levels of human-AI collaboration map the trajectory.

title: Hey creators, stop publishing content. Start publishing your second brain.
description: The subscribable brain model: experts maintain knowledge repos that subscribers integrate into their own AI systems.

title: Workers' "second brains" break every assumption about how we secure knowledge work
description: Personal AI knowledge systems change the security threat model entirely. You must assume everything a worker hears or reads enters their knowledge base within seconds.

title: I built a second brain using AI, and it's changed the way I work. THIS is the future of knowledge work.
description: The anchor article. How a second brain works, why it matters, and why this is the most important shift in knowledge work.

title: Blueprints that actually get built: how change really happens in the enterprise
description: Wharton Tech Conference keynote. Three historical case studies prove the bottleneck is never the technology. Closes with a live second brain demo. Slides available.

title: OpenClaw and Moltbook preview the changes needed with corporate AI governance
description: Worker-built AI tools are emerging faster than governance frameworks can handle. Two real examples of what is coming.

title: Claude Cowork Shows How Unready Workplaces Are for AI Agents
description: Quoted on why Claude's Cowork launch reveals how unprepared enterprise workplaces are for autonomous AI agents.

title: Everyone's worried about the wrong AI security risk
description: The real risk is not secrets leaking into AI models. It is workers building personal knowledge systems that leave with them.

title: The invisible 80%—what corporate-led AI transformations can't see
description: Corporate AI can only reach the 20% of work that is visible. The other 80% lives in workers' heads, and only they can reach it.

title: No AI Bombshells in 2027, Just Reality Catching Up
description: Six predictions for the year: AI as real coworker for a minority, worker-led adoption still ahead, and the skills revolution accelerating.

title: 7 ways to kill IT's value to the business
description: Quoted on the patterns that erode IT's strategic value inside organizations.

title: Workers don't want to build automations. They want to delegate.
description: The 'AI automation studio' vision fails because workers think like managers, not programmers. Delegation beats engineering.

title: AI 2026: When Agents, Browsers, and Security Collide
description: Year-end review on Impact of AI: Explored. CUA benchmarks crossing human parity, AI browsers as security concern, and MCP going ubiquitous.

title: AI will be THE interface to knowledge work. Here's how we'll get there.
description: AI platforms, not apps, become the primary interface to work. The pathway from copilot to workspace to orchestrator.

title: Our vision for AI and the future of work
description: Citrix AI Think Tank, Paris. The pre-November stump speech: AI disappointment paradox, post-application era, workspace as control plane. Slides available.

title: IT admits workers control AI. Workers admit they use it to leave at 5.
description: Microsoft Ignite survey confirms: workers control AI adoption, not IT. And workers keep the productivity gains for themselves.

title: Two futurists from Citrix and Microsoft walk into a bar.
description: Joint session with Microsoft's Simon May at Citrix UNITE, exploring how AI transforms the workspace from two vendor perspectives.

title: Everyone wants to provide your AI. Nobody wants to help you manage it.
description: A typical worker encounters 5+ different AIs daily. The governance gap between AI provision and AI management is widening.

title: AI in the Workplace: What's happening in the next 12 months
description: Keynote at SHI Fall Summit on the trajectory of AI in enterprise IT over the next 12 months.

title: Will AI need to operate your legacy desktop apps, or is direct file manipulation enough?
description: An epiphany: Claude edited an Excel file without opening Excel. What happens when AI does not need the application at all?

title: AI & the future of enterprise apps
description: Closing keynote at AppManagEvent on what happens to enterprise applications as AI makes software creation nearly free.

title: AI just created 10,000 accidental citizen developers in your company. Welcome to the post-application era!
description: The ratio inverted from one app per ten users to ten apps per employee. Software creation costs approaching zero changes everything.

title: The bitter lesson of workplace AI: Stop engineering, start enabling
description: Applying Rich Sutton’s bitter lesson to the enterprise: simple, worker-driven AI adoption beats elaborate IT-engineered solutions. Every time.

description: Interview on converting worker-led AI adoption from a compliance problem into a strategic advantage.

title: If AI is normal technology, boring infrastructure is your best strategy
description: If AI takes years to integrate like every other technology wave, stable and flexible infrastructure beats chasing shiny objects.

description: Axess Connect Part 2: productivity definitions, job compression, career disruption, and precursors to the second brain approach.

title: Worker-led AI isn't shadow IT. It's shadow strategy.
description: Reframing worker-led AI adoption from compliance problem to the real innovation engine that enterprises should enable, not contain.

title: Everyone's wrong about why enterprise AI is failing
description: The transformation McKinsey promises with future agents is being attempted right now by workers with today's consumer AI tools.

title: If AI progress stopped today, we can still transform the enterprise with what we have

title: AI agents are the new insider threat. Secure them like human workers.

description: Exploring what perfect CUA benchmark scores mean for enterprise IT. Agents that can operate any application change the governance model.

title: Why AI agents will use the same desktops and apps as human workers
description: AI operating GUIs is not a bug but a feature. Agents need the same workspace infrastructure as humans.

description: The workspace is not a device, OS, or browser. It is a dynamic abstraction where apps, identity, security, and context converge.

description: Three categories: AI in products, AI used internally, and AI reshaping customers' workplaces. The third is the transformation that matters.

description: AI did not wait for strategy memos. Workers do not care about ROI debates. Here is where to actually start.
